title: Alfalfa cultivar comparison trials  procedure
author: Bortolazzo, Elena
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
This procedure includes a series of measurements to be carried out to measure the performance of the different varieties, cultivars and/or ecotypes of alfalfa used in a comparison trial. 
 This procedure includes the estimation of: 
 
 Alfalfa yield, which is the weight of alfalfa harvested during one year, referred to the dry matter content of the crop, per unit of land harvested (t d.m /ha) (Fischer, 2015). 
 dormancy, is defined as the reduction in shoot growth in the autumn due to decreasing temperatures and day length (Teuber et al., 1998). It is a useful trait that defines alfalfa adaptation to different regions. 
 persistence and longevity, this trait is related to the number of alfalfa vital plants at the end of the season. It is important for the productivity and profitability of the farming system (Burnet et. Al, 2020).
